title: asplenium celtibericum subsp. molinae
intro: Asplenium celtibericum subsp. molinae, also known as Molina's spleenwort, is a rare and endangered subspecies of fern native to the Iberian Peninsula. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Asplenium celtibericum subsp. molinae include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and act as an antioxidant. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are typically harvested and dried to be used in infusions, decoctions, or as a tincture to harness its medicinal properties.
